However, losing 10 Y is also substantial. Let's look at the value. If we assume X and Y are roughly equal in value, the trade is balanced (10 for 10). But wait, I need to check if I can do better or if this is a good deal. I have plenty of Y (25) and very few X (5). The other player has plenty of X (25) and very few Y (5). This is a classic complementarity situation. They need Y, I need X. Trading 10 X for 10 Y seems fair and efficient to reach a more balanced state. Is there a reason to reject?
